title: Can India Build Smarter Heat Wave Warnings?
subtitle: 印度高温预警为何需要更精准？

paragraphs.en: Heat action plans, or HAPs, have been proliferating in India in the past few years. In general, an HAP spells out when and how officials should issue heat warnings and alert hospitals and other institutions. Nagpur's plan, for instance, calls for hospitals to set aside "cold wards" in the summer for treating heatstroke patients, and advises builders to give construction laborers a break from work on very hot days.
paragraphs.zh: 过去几年，高温行动计划在印度迅速推广开来。通常而言，一份高温行动计划会明确规定官员应在何时、以何种方式发布高温预警，并通知医院及其他机构。以那格浦尔市为例，其计划要求医院在夏季预留"低温病房"用于救治中暑患者，并建议建筑商在酷热天气让施工人员暂停作业。

paragraphs.en: But implementation of existing HAPs has been uneven, according to a report from the Centre for Policy Research. Many lack adequate funding, it found. And their triggering thresholds often are not customized to the local climate.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，政策研究中心的一份报告指出，现有高温行动计划的执行情况参差不齐。调查发现，许多计划缺乏充足的资金支持。而且，其触发预警的阈值往往并未根据当地气候条件进行调整。

paragraphs.en: In some areas, high daytime temperatures alone might serve as an adequate trigger for alerts. But in other places, nighttime temperatures or humidity might be as important a gauge of risk as daytime highs. Mumbai's April heat stroke deaths highlighted the need for more nuanced and localized warnings, researchers say.
paragraphs.zh: 在某些地区，仅凭白天高温便可触发预警。但在另一些地区，夜间温度或湿度作为风险评估指标，其重要性可能与日间最高气温不相上下。研究人员指出，孟买四月发生的中暑致死事件便凸显出对更精细、更本地化的预警体系的迫切需求。

paragraphs.en: That day's high temperature of roughly 36°C was 1°C shy of the heat wave alert threshold for coastal cities set by national meteorological authorities. But the effects of the heat were amplified by humidity — an often neglected factor in heat alert systems — and the lack of shade at the late-morning outdoor ceremony. To help improve HAPs, urban planner Kotharkar's team is working on a model plan that outlines best practices and could be adapted to local conditions.
paragraphs.zh: 当日最高气温约为36摄氏度，比国家气象部门针对沿海城市设定的热浪预警阈值低了一度。然而，湿度这一在高温预警系统中常被忽略的因素，加之上午户外仪式现场缺乏遮阳条件，共同加剧了高温的影响。为助力完善高温行动计划，城市规划师科塔卡所带领的团队正在制定一份示范方案，其中归纳了最佳实践并可因地制宜进行调整。

paragraphs.en: Among other things, she says, all cities should create a vulnerability map to help focus responses on the populations most at risk. Such mapping doesn't need to be complex, Kotharkar says. "A useful map can be created by looking at even a few key parameters."
paragraphs.zh: 她表示，除其他举措外，所有城市都应绘制脆弱性地图，以便将应对措施精准聚焦于风险最高的人群。科塔卡称，这种制图工作并不需要多么复杂。"只需考察少数几个关键参数，就能绘制出一张有用的地图。"

paragraphs.en: For example, neighborhoods with a large elderly population or informal dwellings that cope poorly with heat could get special warnings or be bolstered with cooling centers. The Nagpur project has already created a risk and vulnerability map, which enabled Kotharkar to tell officials which neighborhoods to focus on in the event of a heat wave this summer.
paragraphs.zh: 例如，老年人口比例较高的社区，或是抵御高温能力较差的非正规住宅区域，均可接收专门预警或得到降温中心的支持。那格浦尔项目业已绘制出一份风险与脆弱性地图，科塔卡由此得以向官员明确指出，若今夏热浪来袭，应重点关注哪些街区。

paragraphs.en: HAPs shouldn't just include short-term emergency responses, researchers say, but also recommend medium- to long-term measures that could make communities cooler. In Nagpur, for example, Kotharkar's team has been able to advise city officials about where to plant trees to provide shade. HAPs could also guide efforts to retrofit homes or modify building regulations.
paragraphs.zh: 研究人员表示，高温行动计划不应仅涵盖短期应急响应，还应建议采取可使社区整体降温的中长期措施。以那格浦尔为例，科塔卡的团队已能向市政官员就何处植树以提供树荫提出建议。此类计划还可为住宅改造或建筑法规修订提供指引。

paragraphs.en: "Reducing deaths in an emergency is a good target to have, but it's the lowest target," says climate researcher Chandni Singh.
paragraphs.zh: "减少紧急状况中的死亡人数是个好目标，但它只是最低目标。"气候研究员钱德尼·辛格如是说。
